hypothesis: SETI radio transient × EEG cognitive rhythm — pair correlation R2(r) congruence implies cross-domain bisociation (cosmic signal × neural rhythm same Wigner/Poisson statistics)

sedi_source.data: 1977-08-15 Wow! signal Big Ear ch2 extended_context = before(10) + ch2(15) + after(10) = 35 SNR ints (peak SNR=30)

eeg_source.data: anima dual N-back protocol block-level metrics: mean_alpha_correct, mean_gamma_correct, mean_alpha_error, mean_theta_error, mean_rt_ms, accuracy across 3 blocks × 2 sessions

comparison_to_proxy.delta_ks: 25x larger (real spectra have very different scales)
comparison_to_proxy.delta_pearson: -0.753 (proxy artificial high correlation from sorted-similar data)
comparison_to_proxy.lesson: proxy data correlation was artifact of similar sort orders, not real signal coupling

interpretation: real-data verdict shows two domains have fundamentally different spectral character — Wow! signal is single-spike radio transient (35-bin SNR with ~6 nonzero peaks), anima EEG is continuous psychometrics (n=18 block-level alpha/gamma/theta/RT). SEDI mixed verdict (0.501) reflects single-event spike isolated in zero noise; EEG strongly Poisson (0.086) reflects clustered band-power values. composite=0.438 < 0.7 BT threshold — divergent.
next_path_to_breakthrough: aggregate multi-event SETI catalog (Allen Telescope/SETI@home archive) → continuous radio time-series
longitudinal EEG (months of daily protocol_log accumulation) → richer cognitive spectrum
test radio-burst FRB catalog × EEG burst events (matched temporal granularity)
spectral-folding alignment (DTW or multi-scale) before composite
